learning.whatWillYouLearn: Prerequisites: Arrays, Sorting, Strings, Custom Comparator, Greedy Thinking
learning.whyDoesThisExist: Imagine arranging number cards to create the biggest possible number. Instead of simply placing the largest individual numbers first, you compare which pair ordering produces a larger combined number.
learning.realWorldAnalogy: Imagine arranging number cards to create the biggest possible number. Instead of simply placing the largest individual numbers first, you compare which pair ordering produces a larger combined number.
learning.whenToUseThis: Use this pattern whenever elements must be arranged to maximize or minimize a concatenated result using a custom sorting rule.
learning.signals: Whenever numbers or strings must be arranged to maximize or minimize a concatenated value, think of Custom Comparator Sorting.
learning.expectedLearningOutcome: I will know when to use this pattern.

problem.problemStatement: Given a list of non-negative integers nums, arrange them such that they form the largest possible number and return it as a string. Since the result may be very large, return it as a string instead of an integer.

examples.explanation: Sorting using the custom comparator produces the largest possible number.

thinkingProcess.naiveIdea: Generate every possible arrangement and return the largest concatenated number.
thinkingProcess.keyInsight: The decision depends on two numbers together. For any two numbers a and b, compare (a+b) and (b+a). Place them in the order that creates the larger concatenated number.
thinkingProcess.ahaMoment: Compare concatenated strings instead of numeric values.
thinkingProcess.patternRecognition: Whenever numbers or strings must be arranged to maximize or minimize a concatenated value, think of Custom Comparator Sorting.
thinkingProcess.whyItWorks: Sorting with a custom comparator guarantees every adjacent pair contributes optimally to the final concatenated number. No exhaustive search is required.
thinkingProcess.howToRecognizeSimilar: Whenever numbers or strings must be arranged to maximize or minimize a concatenated value, think of Custom Comparator Sorting.
commonWrongPaths: Sorting numerically instead of using concatenation.
Comparing lengths instead of concatenated strings.
Returning an integer instead of a string.

commonMisconceptions: Sorting numerically instead of using concatenation.
Comparing lengths instead of concatenated strings.
Returning an integer instead of a string.

pattern.name: Custom Comparator Sorting
pattern.recognitionSignals: Whenever numbers or strings must be arranged to maximize or minimize a concatenated value, think of Custom Comparator Sorting.

approaches.title: Generate All Permutations
approaches.idea: Generate every possible arrangement and return the largest concatenated number.
approaches.dryRun: Try every possible ordering and keep the largest.

code.java: Permutation solution omitted because of factorial complexity.

approaches.title: Sort Using Custom Comparator
approaches.idea: Convert numbers to strings and sort them based on which concatenation produces the larger number.
algorithm: Convert every number to a string.

approaches.title: Custom Comparator Sorting
approaches.idea: Convert every number into a string and sort them using a custom comparator. For two strings a and b, place a before b if (a+b) is greater than (b+a). Finally concatenate all strings. If the first character is '0', return '0'.
approaches.intuition: Sorting with a custom comparator guarantees every adjacent pair contributes optimally to the final concatenated number. No exhaustive search is required.
algorithm: Convert every integer to a string.
If the first string is '0', return '0'.
Append every string into a StringBuilder.

visualization.description: Sort strings based on which concatenation is larger.
steps: Return '0' if all numbers are zero.

interviewFollowUps: Can this be solved without sorting?
What if negative numbers are allowed?
Why is the answer returned as a string?
interviewTips: Show the a+b vs b+a comparison.
Return the result as a string.

cheatSheet.keyObservation: Compare concatenated strings instead of numeric values.
cheatSheet.algorithm: Convert → Custom Sort → Join → Handle Zero.

commonMistakes: Sorting numerically instead of using concatenation.
Comparing lengths instead of concatenated strings.
Returning an integer instead of a string.

relatedProblems: Largest Number At Least Twice of Others
Kth Largest Element in an Array
